title: Uniface 10.4.03: Updates since 10.4.02
text: Uniface 10.4.03: Updates since 10.4.02 Uniface 10.4.03 consolidates new features, bug fixes and currency updates since 10.4.02. Documentation was updated for the following: 10.4 .02.052 Obsolete Database Connector settings: The open statements setting is now obsolete The open statements setting is obsolete and should not be used anymore. The open statements setting is now accepted in the assignment file only for compatibility reasons. 10.4 .02.051 Feature: The regex or syntax pattern length limit for field validation is extended The regex or syntax pattern length limit for field validation is extended from 31 to 192. For more information, see Field Syntax and Field Syntax Codes . Feature: Added configurable setting to enable the Enter key for selecting auto-complete suggestions A new setting completiononenter is available in the ProcScript Editor ( usys.ini ) to enable or disable the auto-completion acceptance with the Enter key. For more details, see Code completion settings . 10.4 .02.048 Feature: Regular expression is available as a new field entry format as well as in Proc functions Regex string is introduced as a new entry format for user input and pattern matching. A regex string is a regular expression enclosed in forward slashes (‘ / ’). Regex strings can be used to specify a field syntax, such as postal code or telephone number. They are also useful when searching for and comparing or replacing strings. For more information, see Entry Format , Regex Strings for Pattern Matching , and $regex . Feature: A new configuration assignment settings $DSP_FIELDLIST is added for compatibility switch Added $DSP_FIELDLIST as a new compatibility switch that allows you to also use the FieldList. When $DSP_FIELDLIST is set to 1, the fields included in the DSP client communication are based upon the field lists as it is generated by the compiler. If $DSP_FIELDLIST is set to 0, only fields included in the component structure are included in the communication. For more details, see $DSP_FIELDLIST , [SETTINGS] , and Field Lists . 10.4 .02.047 Feature: Sentinel new license options for improving Redundant License checkout speed Sentinel RMS servers were very slow in responding to feature checkouts when redundant RMS servers are being used. To resolve this problem, two new license option settings are introduced in the $license_options settings, LM_REDUNDANT and LM_TIMEOUT . For more details, see LM_REDUNDANT and LM_TIMEOUT . 10.4 .02.045 Feature: Format ProcScript, indentation corrections on entire code document With Format Document , you can correct or improve your code indentation to increase code readability and understanding of the entire code document. You can either use the Format Document option from the popup menu or use the default shortcut key Alt+Shift+F defined in the usys.ini file. For more details, see ProcScript code formatting , [userkeys] and Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE . Feature: Configurable formatting rules As different organizations may use different code formatting conventions, Uniface provides settings to configure the formatting rules. It also allows you to enforce the usage of the same rules across your organization. For more details, see Enforcing ProcScript Code Conventions , [FORMATTING] , and Configure Indentation in the Script Editor . Doc Enhancement: Uniface UX widget properties documentation Added documentation for all released Uniface UX physical and logical widget properties. For more details, see Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages , Physical Widgets , and Logical Widgets . 10.4 .02.043 Feature: Format ProcScript, indentation corrections on selected code With Format Selection , you can correct or improve your code indentation to increase code readability and understanding. You can either use the Format Selection option from the popup menu or use the default shortcut key Alt+F defined in the usys.ini file. For more details, see ProcScript code formatting , [userkeys] and Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE . 10.4 .02.041 Feature: Auto completion pop-up list for Uniface Proc language statements, functions, and pre-compiler directives When you start typing in the code editor, the IDE displays a small pop-up list containing Uniface proc language statements, functions, and pre-compiler directives. The auto-complete feature does not interfere with your typing action. You can always press the Esc key to close the auto-completion list. For more details, see Code completion settings and [widgets] . 10.4 .02.038 Feature: Enable bi-directional writing for an RTL language Right-to-left (RTL) languages are written from right to left of the screen, such as Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian languages. By default, bi-directional writing is disabled in Uniface IDE editor. To enable bi-directional writing for an RTL language, you need to update the usys.ini file. For more details, see Enabling bi-directional writing for an RTL language . Feature: Configure auto-indent feature Auto-indent is an automated feature in the Uniface Script Editor that automatically adjusts the indentation of Proc lines, ensuring consistent and readable formatting. This feature is enabled by default and can be disabled by setting autoindent=off in the usys.ini file. For more details, see Configuring the auto-indentation feature . Doc update: Restructured Uniface library table of content (TOC) for a few newly released features Restructured TOC for some of the sections in the Uniface library. For more details, see Uniface UX and Configure the Script Editor . 10.4 .02.037 Feature: Uniface UX - the out of the box Uniface user experience An excellent User Experience (UX) makes your customers want to use your application. Uniface UX offers you an out of the box experience allowing you to modernize the user experience of your application. Uniface UX provides field level widgets to directly influence your user experience. Over the coming Uniface releases, Uniface UX will be enhanced to include new constructs. You add these constructs on top of your existing business model, allowing you to improve the user experience while you continue to focus on your business model, data, rules, and the business flow of your application. For more details, see Uniface UX , and Enable and use . Feature: Uniface UX Widgets Important: This is an experimental feature and is subject to change. Uniface UX Widgets is a set of pre-built widgets for DSP fields and is the first deliverable of more pre-built constructs yet to be developed. It offers a consistent look and feel, advanced widget options, simplifying DSP development and maintenance. These standard widgets are built using Uniface's UX Interface and can be duplicated and extended with extra functionality. The Uniface UX widgets expose Microsoft's Fluent web components for use in DSPs. To enable the UX Widgets, uncomment their definitions in the usys.ini. The UX Widgets are identified by their UX prefix. For more details, see UX Widgets . Feature: UX Interface UX Interface is the collection of enabling APIs allowing users to build a consistent UX/UI. The UX Interface opens up parts of Uniface needed to efficiently interact with the core of Uniface, while providing proper isolation of concerns keeping the development of custom constructs easy and maintainable. Feature: UX Widget Interface Important: This is an experimental feature and is subject to change. The UX Widget Interface allows extending DSP development by enabling encapsulation of UI and client/server UX code into reusable widgets. As part of the Interface, Rocket Uniface provides tutorials and training material, allowing you to learn the creation of your own widget in a step-by-step approach. For more details, see UX Widget Interface , Creating a UX Widget , UX Widget States and API Functions , and UX Widget Troubleshooting Checklist . To learn about creating a UX Widget in detail, enrol into Uniface UX e-learning course: Uniface UX (rocketsoftware.com) . Feature: A new ProcScript function $random has been introduced A new random ProcScript function $random has been introduced with three modes: raw , string , and numeric . Raw creates fixed-length raw data helpful in creating cryptographically secure initialization vectors, string creates fixed-length strings of random characters, and numeric generates a random number between 0 and 1. For more details, see $random , numeric , and $length . Feature: Doc update for libcurl 8.4.0 upgrade The document is updated for libcurl 8.4.0 upgrade. For more details, see SEND . 10.4 .02.034 Feature: Base64URL encoding support for simplified data handling Base64URL encoding is a variant of the traditional Base64 encoding with better URL safety. It allows you to represent binary data safely in text format, making it ideal for use in URLs, JSON, and other contexts where you need to avoid certain characters. With Base64URL encoding supported by $encode and $decode, you can seamlessly exchange data in a more efficient and secure manner. For more details, see $encode and $decode . Feature: Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) support for enhanced security Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) is an approach for asymmetric key cryptography, offering versatile applications in both encryption and digital signatures. It offers strong security with shorter key lengths compared to other asymmetric cryptographic algorithms. For more details, see Elliptic Curve Cryptography , $encode , and $decode . 10.4 .02.033 Feature: Enhanced keyboard shortcut support for compiling and testing Development Objects in the IDE You can define or use the default keyboard shortcut key combinations for compiling and testing objects in the Uniface IDE. For more details, see Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE and Compile a Project . Doc update: [userkeys] section in the usys.ini file Improved documentation instructions on [userkeys]. For more details, see [userkeys] and Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE . 10.4 .02.032 Feature: Enhanced Uniface Accessibility feature Uniface exposes the UI of generated Windows desktop applications to screen readers and automation tools for a number of widgets. By exposing the underlying infrastructure, screen readers and automated testing tools can target supported UI elements, and retrieve information regarding their name, value, and status. For these purposes, a mixture of two different technologies are supported - UI Automation and MSAA (Microsoft Active Accessibility). For more details, see Accessibility . Feature: Using Expressions in Assignment Files Assignment files are used to configure the Uniface applications. To solve the problem of maintaining almost identical assignment files, you can use environment variables and expressions in Uniface assignment files. For more details, see Using Expressions in Assignment Files . Feature: Enhanced keyboard shortcut support for easy navigation inside the editor For easy and quick navigation inside the editor, keyboard shortcuts can be used. For more details, see Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE . 10.4 .02.031 Feature: Uppercase and lowercase code You can uppercase and lowercase code in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click and click Uppercase on the popup menu to uppercase the current selection. Similarly, you need to right-click inside a selection and click Lowercase to lowercase a selected text. For more details, see Uppercase and lowercase code . Enhancement: Comment and uncomment code Instructions to add shortcut keys defined for Uppercase and Lowercase feature in the [userkeys] section are updated. For more details, see Comment and uncomment code . 10.4 .02.030 Feature: Comment and uncomment code You can comment and uncomment codes in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click on a code and click Comment on the popup menu to comment out a line when there is no selection or a complete selection of codes. Similarly, you need to right-click inside the commented code or a selection and click Uncomment to remove a comment from the code and make the code part of the program flow. For more details, see Comment and uncomment code . 10.4 .02.028 Feature: New ULIC command ulic /info is added ulic /info provides information about an entitlement, such as the number of activations that are left, the features it licenses, and the list of associated friendly names. For more details, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Feature: Add multiple (modeled) fields onto the canvas of a Form/Report You can insert or paste multiple fields into a component structure using this feature. For more details, see Define Frames Worksheet , Copy and Paste Objects and Text , Add Objects from the Resource Browser , and Define the Structure and Layout of Forms and Reports . Doc update: $LICENSE_OPTIONS Improved instructions and structure of the $LICENSE_OPTIONS section. For more details, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . 10.4 .02.027 Feature: Implemented escape character mechanism to fix SOP 2.0 basic authentication password parsing issue Escape characters that might get treated as part of the password parameter, both for ppass and epass. For more details, see SOAP Connector Options . Doc update: $LICENSE_OPTIONS Improved instructions and structure of the $LICENSE_OPTIONS section. For more details, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . Doc update: trigger userKey Improved documentation instructions on supported userkeys. For more details, see trigger userKey . Doc update: Pre-Start Uniface Servers Improved documentation instructions on PRE_START settings. For more details, see Pre-Start Uniface Servers and [PRE_START] . 10.4 .02.025 Feature: Added keyboard shortcut support on Windows for the Uniface applications without menubar Uniface developers can define and use keyboard shortcuts within a Uniface application. This feature will enhance the work productivity of developers. For more details, see trigger userKey . 10.4 .02.023 Feature: A new license notification option LM_LOGFILE is added A new license notification option LM_LOGFILE added to enable or disable licenses expiry notifications in the log file. The notifications will start appearing in the log file before the defined number of days from the maximum release date. For more details, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . 10.4 .02.022 Doc update: Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing Improved instructions of the Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing topic. For more details, see Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing . Doc update: ProcScript Module for breakpoints Updated instructions in the ProcScript Module for breakpoints topic. For more details, see ProcScript Module for breakpoints . Doc update: throw Improved instructions of the throw topic. For more details, see throw . 10.4 .02.021 Doc update: Enabling BASIC authentication for Uniface Web Application A note has been added to explain how BASIC authentication stops working when disabled in a Web browser. Additionally, cause for Error: 212 is also updated. For more details, see Authentication for Uniface Web Applications and Browser Displays a Red Error Page . Feature: Create private keys using OpenSSL from the Uniface bin on Windows You can now create a private key using OpenSSL from the Uniface bin by providing the configuration file path. For more details, see Configuring Uniface Applications to Use TLS . Feature: Enhanced maximum allowed characters in a line in an .ini file The maximum allowed characters in a line in an .ini file is enhanced from 512 characters to 4000 characters. For more details, see usys.ini . 10.4 .02.019 Feature : New Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) 7.1 is released with the following feature: Supports HTTP method PATCH For more details, see Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) , INPUT , REST-Compliant Web Services , Supported Media Types , SEND , UHTTP , and Web Services . Feature: LDAP driver security enhancement on UNIX The new net_profile settings defined in the Uniface ASN file helps in improving the security of the LDAP driver on UNIX. For more details, see net_profile (UNIX only) and LDAP over TLS . 10.4 .02.017 Feature: New Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) 7.0 is released with the following features: Supports SameSite cookie attributes. Supports Servlet API 6.0, which thus works with Servlet 6.0 compliant Web servers, such as Tomcat 10.1. For more details, see DEFAULTSAMESITE , $webinfo: Cookie Topics , web.xml , and Enabling SameSite Cookie value for Web Server . Feature: SOAP driver security enhancement The new min_tls_version and the cipher_suite driver settings are added for the SOAP driver for improving security. The new settings allow you as a system administrator to set the minimum required protocol and cipher for the network connection. For more details, see SOAP Connector Options . Feature: Enhanced Uniface Debugger features The enhanced debugger Error breakpoint settings allow developers to break at specific Proc errors as well as exceptions. There are also improvements in the behavior of the debugger during debug sessions. For more details, see Working with Error and Exception related breakpoints , Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior , Uniface Debugger (Online Help) , Working with breakpoints , Breakpoints , and Configuring the Uniface Debugger . Feature: New EnableDevTools HTML widget property is added EnableDevTools is an HTML widget property that allows a user to enable the DevTools (Developer Tools) of Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF). When a component is compiled with /nodebug , it deactivates the DebugPort and EnableDevTools properties, irrespective of what is set in the .INI file. For more details, see EnableDevTools (HTML Widget) , DebugPort , and /nodebug . 10.4 .02.016 Feature: UHTTP connection settings can use SET_PROFILE rather than SET_FLAGS Uniface supports a new function, SET_PROFILE , to control communication and security parameters in UHTTP components that SEND messages. The previous approached used the SET_FLAGS options, but the new profile method provides additional options and is the recommended approach. See the related documentation for SET_PROFILE and UHTTP for details. Feature: A new ProcScript module type 'function' has been introduced Uniface now supports a new ProcScript module type function . The function module can help you in keeping code more self-contained. You can still use the Entry module type as a component-level function. See the related documentation Functions and function...end for details. 10.4 .02.015 Doc maintenance: The new Uniface documentation library and online help is live This release is the first time the Uniface IDE is linking to the Rocket Software Documentation Portal for context sensitive help as the primary documentation repository. There are several new features in the portal which are explained in a short video on the Uniface Youtube channel. To help you make the transition, we are keeping the former Uniface library available (last updated for 10.4.02.008) for some time and have added a banner that indicates new feature documentation is included only in the new Rocket Uniface Library for 10.4 . You can still use the local .chm file as needed and it reflects the same content as the current online library. 10.4 .02.013 Feature: ULIC qualifiers /typ added for /act and /ret Qualifiers for /act and /ret , that is /typ={standalone | network} is added. This will cause ulic /act to only activate the entitlement for the specified license type. If an incorrect entitlement ID is provided, it will produce an error and will not activate the entitlement. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) , Install the Uniface Enterprise Edition on Windows , Installing Uniface on Unix and Linux , Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Windows , and Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . 10.4 .02.012 Feature: TLS 1.0. and 1.1 are now disabled by default Uniface now provides additional settings to enhance the security of the application. Using the TLS driver options or the network profile, you can enable the specific unsafe protocols, such as TLS 1.0 and 1.1. For more information, see TLS Connector Options and min_tls_version . Feature: A new TLS profile option has been added enabling the ability to prevent renegotiation Uniface now provides settings to disable renegotiation of the network connection while maintaining the current feature of enabling or disabling ciphers. For more information, see TLS Connector Options and renegotiation . Issue: Remove “cloud-served” from the Grace licenses page in the Uniface documentation library The “cloud-served” text was wrongly added in the first sentence of the Grace Licenses page. The text is removed now, and the documentation is updated. For more information, see Grace Licenses . Issue: Uniface DB2 connector U4.1 for DB2 11 on Windows: mappings exceed limitations of DB2 SQL Uniface did not consider DB2 Data Packing limitations earlier. This resulted in exceeding the mapping limitations. The issue is resolved, and the documentation is updated. For more information, see DB2 Data Packing . Feature: Enable ULIC to disassociate the unused fingerprints from Entitlement ID and Customer ID Firstly, the ULIC /ret command issue is resolved. The ULIC /ret command now disassociates the fingerprint registered from the Entitlement ID and the Customer ID as part of the return process. Secondly, a new ULIC switch ULIC /delfn=<entitlement id> is implemented. The new ULIC /delfn command enables our customers, who use the Sentinel EMS web portal, to return the licenses, and disassociate the registered fingerprints from Entitlement ID and Customer ID. The new ULIC /delfn also enables us to clean up the existing unused fingerprints in our database. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Feature: Sentinel version 10 is now available for AIX and iSeries (without cloud support) On the $LICENSE_OPTIONS page, the LM_LOAD option is documented. The LM_LOAD option specifies how Uniface tries to check out Sentinel licenses. LM_LOAD=10, that is Sentinel version 10, is now supported on all platforms, such as Windows, Linux, AIX, and iSeries. For AIX and iSeries, cloud deployments are still not supported. For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . Issue: UROUTMON: maxidle and maxsrv values > 32767 were not correctly shown When a UST has a /max ( maxsrv ; the maximum number of server instances per UST) or /maxidle (maximum idle time in seconds for a server of a UST) value > 32,767 defined, then the corresponding values in the Uniface Router Monitor Servers window or the values returned by the UROUTMON component operation GET_SERVERS are not correct. This issue is resolved, and the respective documentation is updated. For more information, see UROUTER_SET_UST and GET_SERVERS . Feature enhancement: The maximum $TIMEOUT limit is increased We have extended the timeout limit. Now you can set a maximum $TIMEOUT value of 10080 minutes, which equals one week time. For more information, see $TIMEOUT . Feature: Stop saving the application shell position when the application closes and history=off Uniface always saves the application shell position and size in the Windows registry. Sometimes this is not required or wanted. An additional property has been added to the application shell to stop storing such details. Both $applproperties and shell properties have been extended with position and size properties to force the application to start-up at a certain position and size. Read the Shell and $applproperties documentation for details. Documentation library: Migrating to a new documentation library platform with rich features We will be moving to a new documentation library platform soon. The new doc platform has multiple features that will help our Uniface customers in using the Uniface documentation more effectively. Stay tuned! For more information about the new documentation platform, see New Uniface documentation library introduction . 10.4 .02.008 Uniface Docker images now support Cloud LM licensing You can now use a Cloud License Manager (LM) Entitlement to license your Uniface Server Docker image. For more information, see Cloud License Manager (LM) Entitlement and Deploying the Uniface Server Docker Image . 10.4 .02.007 Issue: The goto statement and label are not allowed in a try, catch, or finally block Do not use either the goto statement or ProcScript label inside a try , catch , or finally block. This results in a compilation error. For more information, see try in the Uniface Library. Feature: The Load Definitions facility is now available for SQLite, Sybase, and MySQL The Project Editor has been enhanced to support the Load Definitions facility for SQLite, Sybase, and MySQL. This enables you to create modeled entities using the definitions of your existing SQLite, Sybase, or MySQL databases. For more information, see Load Definitions Facility for SQLite , Load Definitions Facility for Sybase , and Load Definitions Facility for MySQL . Doc Maintenance: SQLite Data Packing has been enhanced The table describing how Uniface packing codes are mapped to SQLite storage formats has been enhanced to include SQLite typenames. For more information, see SQLite Data Packing in the Uniface Library. 10.4 .02.006 Feature: All modules can now bubble up exceptions An exception caused by code that is exception-enabled will now always bubble up the call stack until it is caught by a corresponding try-catch block or exit as an uncaught exception. Previously a module needed a throws declaration in order to raise an exception to its caller. With this change, every module can raise an exception that bubbles up and pass it up the call stack, even if a module has no throws declaration or try block surrounding the module. For more information, see Exception Handling and throws . 10.4 .02.005 Feature: New rethrow ProcScript statement for Exception Handling The rethrow statement rethrows an exception that was caught in a catch block. This is useful when you want to handle an exception at a different level to which it was caught. rethrow allows you to catch the exception and rethrow it, for a caller to handle it at a higher level. For more information, see rethrow . Doc Maintenance: Configuring a Redundant RMS License Manager pool with a cloud-served license Instructions have been added to configure a redundant RMS License Manager pool with a network cloud-served license. A redundant RMS pool uses redundant RMS License Managers to ensure your licensing system is always online. To configure this, your Sentinel Entitlement needs to include the Redundant RMS feature. Contact your Uniface representative to add this to your network cloud-served license. For more information, see Redundant RMS License Manager Pool . 10.4 .02.004 Doc Maintenance: Enhanced information on Sentinel License Types The overview of Sentinel License Types has been updated with more information on the Entitlement Certificate you receive when registering with Uniface, and the available variations of the license model. For more information, see Sentinel License Types . 10.4 .02.003 Feature: ULIC can now repair persistent storage The Uniface License Management utility (ULIC) now runs repair code if creating persistent storage fails. You can also run ULIC with the /repair flag on its own or with a separate flag for each type of persistent storage. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Feature: New finally ProcScript statement for Exception Handling The finally declaration marks ProcScript inside a try-endtry block that must be executed, regardless of whether an exception occurs or not. This is useful to execute cleanup activities, such as data cleanup or closing a connection, that should occur irrespective of any exceptions. For more information, see finally . 10.4 .02.002 Issue 29459: ULIC now has a timeout when contacting the Sentinel Cloud Server The Uniface License Management utility (ULIC) now has a default timeout of 2 minutes, if it fails to retrieve a response when connecting to the Sentinel Cloud Server to activate or return a license. You can change the length of this timeout, for example if you have slow network speeds, by adding the ULIC_TIMEOUT logical and a valid number to your ulic.asn file. For more information, see ULIC_TIMEOUT . Feature: The USYSHTTP function to return the request handler is now customizable The USYSHTTP component function that returns the request handler is now customizable. vRequestHandler=getHandler() gets the request handler based on the component type of the requested component. You can modify this to add your own way to identify the request handler. Previously when the requested component was not valid, $procerror -50 ( <UACTERR_NO_SIGNATURE> ) was returned. With this change, if the component is not valid, $procerror -156 ( <UACTERR_WRONG_IMPLEMENTATION> ) is returned instead. As the USYSHTTP component source code is now provided, you can customize the ProcScript error sent from the USYSHTTP. For more information, see USYSHTTP . Doc Maintenance: USYSHTTP Global ProcScript library The customizable USYSHTTP component is accompanied with a USYSHTTP Global ProcScript library. The modules in this library provide helper functions for the USYSHTTP component, including functions specific to the DSP protocol. These modules cannot be edited or debugged, however you can replace these functions with your own to customize the behavior before and after handling web requests, and create robust web request handlers. The high-level function of these functions is now documented. For more information, see USYSHTTP . 10.4 .02.001 Feature: $componenttype has been enhanced to return component name The $componenttype function has been enhanced to return the component type based on the component name. To maintain compatibility, the component instance name is used first to get the component type. If an instance was not created, the component name will be used instead to return the component type. Previously, if the instance was not found, <UPROCERR_INSTANCE> was returned. This is no longer the case. If you used $componenttype to check whether a component is already instantiated, please use $componentname instead. For more information, see $componenttype . Feature: Uniface Server Docker Image for containerized deployment We now provide a Uniface Server Docker Image that allows you to run Uniface solutions in a containerized Docker environment. It contains a Linux Uniface server environment, including the Uniface Router and Uniface Server. This image is based on the Red Hat Linux Universal Base Image 8 Minimal distribution that is designed for applications containing their own dependencies. For more information, see Uniface Server Docker Image . You can find an online course on How to Deploy Uniface Solutions using Docker on the Rocket® Uniface elearning site ( https://learn.rocketsoftware.com/ ). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
